model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: NC State holds a slight edge in both KenPom (#34 vs #37) and Torvik (#38 vs #45) rankings, driven primarily by a meaningfully better adjusted defense (104.5 vs 106.0). While the Wolfpack have struggled down the stretch, losing seven of their last nine, Texas has been equally inconsistent with an 18-14 record and an early SEC Tournament exit as a 10-seed to Ole Miss. NC State's superior defensive efficiency and better overall record, combined with their proven tournament pedigree from their 2024 Final Four run as an 11-seed, give them a narrow but real advantage in this play-in matchup.

reasoningStep.title: NC State vs Texas First Four Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: NC State edges Texas in advanced metrics and defensive efficiency despite both teams entering with late-season struggles. The Wolfpack's slightly higher KenPom and Torvik rankings, better defensive numbers, and tournament experience tip the balance in a close matchup.
evidence: NC State KenPom #34 (AdjEM +19.58, AdjO 124.1 #19, AdjD 104.5), Torvik #38, record 20-13 ACC
Texas KenPom #37 (AdjEM +19.06, AdjO 125.0 #13, AdjD 106.0), Torvik #45, record 18-14 SEC
NC State lost 7 of last 9 but beat Pittsburgh 98-88 in ACC Tournament before falling to Virginia 81-74
Texas lost to Ole Miss 76-66 as 10-seed in SEC Tournament; ranks 259th in points allowed (76.8 PPG)
NC State's defensive edge (AdjD 104.5 vs 106.0) is the key differentiator in an otherwise tight matchup

In 2025-26, the Longhorns are 50th in college basketball in rebounds (34.8 per game) and 10th-best in rebounds allowed (26.9). This season Texas is ranked 290th in the nation in assists at 12.3 per game. The Longhorns are 159th in the country in turnovers per game (10.7) and 17th-worst in turnovers forced (9). Texas is 208th in the nation in 3-pointers made (7.4 per game) and 99th in 3-point percentage (35.4%). In 2025-26, the Longhorns are 82nd in the country in 3-pointers conceded (7 per game) and 321st in defensive 3-point percentage (36%). result.text: How to watch Texas Longhorns vs. NC State Wolfpack: NCAA Tournament First Four live stream info, TV channel, game time | March 17 Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Recruiting SEC Sports Odds Newsletter College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports How to watch Texas Longhorns: NCAA Tournament First Four live stream info, TV channel, game time | March 17 Data Skrive Watch as the NC State Wolfpack (20-13) try to earn a spot in the Round of 64 in the NCAA Tournament bracket in their First Four matchup with the Texas Longhorns (18-14) on Tuesday at UD Arena, tipping off at 9:15 p.m. ET, airing on truTV. Ahead of watching this matchup, here's what you need to know about Tuesday's college basketball action. NC State vs. Texas: How to watch on TV or live stream Game day: Tuesday, March 17, 2026 Game time: 9:15 p.m. ET Location: Dayton, Ohio Arena: UD Arena TV Channel: truTV Live Stream: Sling - Watch Now! Check out: USA TODAY Sports Coaches Poll Watch the NCAA Tournament on Sling! NC State vs. Texas stats and trends Texas scores 83.8 points per game and allow 76.8, making them 26th in college basketball on offense and 259th defensively. In 2025-26, the Longhorns are 50th in college basketball in rebounds (34.8 per game) and 10th-best in rebounds allowed (26.9). This season Texas is ranked 290th in the nation in assists at 12.3 per game. The Longhorns are 159th in the country in turnovers per game (10.7) and 17th-worst in turnovers forced (9). Texas is 208th in the nation in 3-pointers made (7.4 per game) and 99th in 3-point percentage (35.4%). In 2025-26, the Longhorns are 82nd in the country in 3-pointers conceded (7 per game) and 321st in defensive 3-point percentage (36%). Texas attempts 35.9% percent of its shots from beyond the arc, and 26.2% of its made baskets are from there. Inside the arc, it attempts 64.1% of its shots, with 73.8% of its makes coming from there. modelTrace.content:
Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me compile my analysis:

**NC State (20-13, ACC)**
- KenPom: #34, AdjEM +19.58, AdjO 124.1 (#19), AdjD 104.5
- Torvik: #38, AdjOE 124.1 (#22), AdjDE 104.6 (#90)
- Lost 7 of last 9 games per ESPN
- Beat Pittsburgh 98-88 in ACC Tournament, then lost to Virginia 81-74
- 20-13 record, ACC conference

**Texas (18-14, SEC)**
- KenPom: #37, AdjEM +19.06, AdjO 125 (#13), AdjD 106
- Torvik: #45, AdjOE 124.1 (#21), AdjDE 106.3 (#125)
- Lost to Ole Miss 76-66 in SEC Tournament (10th seed)
- 18-14 record, SEC conference
- Early-season injury concerns with big men
- Scores 83.8 PPG, allows 76.8 PPG (259th defensively)

Key observations:
- NC State has slightly better overall ratings (KenPom #34 vs #37, Torvik #38 vs #45)
- NC State has notably better defense (AdjD 104.5 vs 106.0)
- Both teams have similar offensive efficiency
- NC State has a better record (20-13 vs 18-14)
- NC State is struggling late (7 of last 9 losses) but Texas also lost early in SEC Tournament
- Texas plays in the stronger conference (SEC) but was a 10-seed there
- NC State has recent tournament pedigree (2024 Final Four as 11-seed)

While the Wolfpack have struggled down the stretch, losing seven of their last nine, Texas has been equally inconsistent with an 18-14 record and an early SEC Tournament exit as a 10-seed to Ole Miss. NC State's superior defensive efficiency and better overall record, combined with their proven tournament pedigree from their 2024 Final Four run as an 11-seed, give them a narrow but real advantage in this play-in matchup."

NC State lost 7 of last 9 but beat Pittsburgh 98-88 in ACC Tournament. Texas lost to Ole Miss 76-66 as 10-seed in SEC Tournament. Texas ranks 259th in points allowed (76.8 PPG).
